title: Ollama Tier 1 Capability Audit
revision_note: Post-W1 correction: Kyle identified contaminated Ollama state during initial probes (resident Qwen + orphan runner). Clean-state retests performed for R2 and R11. GLM full-GPU still fails on clean state (CUDA OOM) but loads with num_gpu=30 (partial offload). Template leak confirmed stop-token fixable. R5 promoted to meets. Fitness report and probe artifacts updated.

acceptance_checks.evidence: AHO_ITERATION=0.2.15 set before emit_workstream_start. Event logged at 2026-04-13T17:43:45Z with iteration=0.2.15.
Checkpoint shows current_workstream=W1, W1=pending_audit. W0=workstream_complete preserved.

acceptance_checks.check: All 12 requirements probed with evidence (no unknown)
acceptance_checks.evidence: 12 probe artifacts in ollama-probes/R01-R12. R02-clean.json and R11-diagnostic.json added during correction. Classifications after correction: 8 meets, 3 partial, 0 fails. R5 promoted to meets after GLM partial-offload routing confirmed.

acceptance_checks.check: Critical requirements (R2, R5, R6, R11) have explicit shippability decisions
acceptance_checks.evidence: R2: partial (GLM needs num_gpu=30, 3 models fully loadable). R5: meets (all 4 route correctly with appropriate config). R6: meets (no context leak). R11: partial (GLM template leak stop-token fixable, W2 scope). All documented in corrected fitness report.

acceptance_checks.check: Any fails documented with workaround or defer notation
acceptance_checks.evidence: No hard fails. Three partial classifications with workarounds: R2 (GLM num_gpu=30), R7 (client-side validation), R11 (W2 stop-token fix + prefix strip).

acceptance_checks.check: Raw response inspection for ambiguous probes
acceptance_checks.evidence: Qwen empty responses diagnosed (thinking-mode). GLM CUDA OOM confirmed via journalctl crash logs. GLM template leak diagnosed with 4 probes (no stops, both stops, end-only stop, identity). Thinking field language inspected.

acceptance_checks.check: baseline_regression_check clean (12 carried from W0, 11 observed)
acceptance_checks.evidence: 11 failed, 304 passed, 1 skipped (1401.74s). W0 baseline: 12 failures, 303 passed. Delta: one fewer failure, one more pass. No new failures introduced. W1 made no src/aho/ code changes.
W1 produced no new code. All artifacts are JSON probe data and markdown. Pre-existing G083 site at workstream_events.py:70-71 not affected.
Pillar 11 respected throughout W1 and correction.

acceptance_checks.check: W1 correction: R2 clean-state retest completed
acceptance_checks.evidence: R02-clean.json written with 7-step LRU sequence + GLM empty-VRAM test + GLM partial-offload test. GLM CUDA OOM root cause identified from journalctl (cudaMalloc failed allocating 1704 MiB). GLM loads with num_gpu=30 at 7239 MiB VRAM.

acceptance_checks.check: W1 correction: R11 GLM template diagnostic completed
acceptance_checks.evidence: R11-diagnostic.json written with 4 probes. Template leak confirmed stop-token fixable. W2 fix path: add <|end_of_box|> to GLM stops + strip <|begin_of_box|> prefix. GLM thinking field present (Chinese for identity, English for classification).

acceptance_checks.check: W1 correction: No checkpoint advance, no workstream_complete
acceptance_checks.evidence: Checkpoint remains at W1/pending_audit. No workstream_start or workstream_complete events emitted during correction.

findings.description: GLM-4.6V-Flash-9B cannot load with full GPU offload (41 layers) on 8GB VRAM. CUDA OOM: model buffer 5539 MiB + CUDA runtime ~1.2 GiB exceeds available VRAM. Loads with num_gpu=30 (partial CPU offload) at 7239 MiB, inference ~36s.

W1 baseline was contaminated during initial probes by resident Qwen + orphan runner process from crashed probe. Clean-state retests performed post-hoc for R2 and R11. Initial '3362 MiB base overhead' figure was actually Nemotron model weights + Ollama runtime, not base.
Qwen 3.5:9B thinking-mode consumes ~150-200 internal tokens per response. num_predict must be >= 2000 for adequate visible output. W2 dispatcher should account for this.
Ollama clean-state VRAM: ~704 MiB (including desktop GPU processes). Ollama process base adds ~449 MiB when runner starts. Available for models: ~7039 MiB.
GLM thinking field contains Chinese reasoning even when output is in English. Present in Ollama 0.20.2 /api/chat responses. W4 cascade consideration.
Nemotron auto-loads on Ollama restart and after model evictions. Cannot be permanently suppressed via keep_alive:0. W2 dispatcher should explicit-unload before loading large models.
GLM CUDA OOM crash kills ALL loaded models — VRAM drops to baseline. Destructive to concurrent model state. W2 dispatcher must verify model presence after GLM operations.
Ollama is system-level systemd service (/etc/systemd/system/ollama.service). Restart requires sudo. install.fish and harness docs should reflect this.
GLM template leak is stop-token fixable. Use <|end_of_box|> as stop + strip <|begin_of_box|> prefix. Confirmed via R11-diagnostic.json probe 4.

kyle_escalations.title: GLM Partial Offload for Tier 1 (was: GLM Non-Functional)
kyle_escalations.description: GLM loads with num_gpu=30 (partial CPU offload). Inference ~36s (vs ~2s for smaller models). Full GPU offload crashes with CUDA OOM even on clean state. Decision: accept GLM with num_gpu=30 in Tier 1? Performance acceptable for Auditor role?
options: Accept GLM with num_gpu=30 in Tier 1
Test optimal num_gpu value in W2

kyle_escalations.title: Ollama Service Layer
kyle_escalations.description: Ollama is system-level systemd. Restart requires sudo. install.fish and harness docs should be updated.

classification_summary.note: R5 promoted from partial to meets after GLM partial-offload routing confirmed.

cross_project_contamination.notes: No cross-project contamination observed. All probe artifacts reference aho-native constructs only.
